growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: ion chromatography; N loading rate 0.48-1.02 kgN/m3/d, N removal 0.42-0.94 kgN/m3/d over 269 d MBR run

yields.note: biomass 1.45 gBSA/L total, planktonic 1.13 gBSA/L at 215 d; Ks(NO2-)=0.47±0.3 mgN/L (Hanes-Woolf); mu_max=0.0984 d^-1 (cited from ref 31)

growth_records.cite: Oshiki, Mamoru et al. Cultivation of Planktonic Anaerobic Ammonium Oxidation (Anammox) Bacteria Using Membrane Bioreactor. Microbes and Environments (2013). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME13077. [PMC4070702]
growth_records.method: reported (net specific growth rate mu_net=0.0232 d^-1 from slope of planktonic-cell exponential proliferation, days 41-215; cross-checked 0.017-0.033 d^-1 from biomass retention time control); mu (gross)=0.0261-0.0314 d^-1 via Monod eq; decay b=0.0029-0.0081 d^-1
growth_records.snippet: An MBR was continuously operated for 269 d with nitrogen loading and nitrogen removal rates of 0.48-1.02 and 0.42 to 0.94 kgN m-3 d-1 ... mu_net was determined to be 0.0232 d-1. ... the value of mu was calculated to be 0.0261-0.0314 d-1 ...
growth_records.notes: Prokaryotic chemolithoautotroph (anammox). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.0232 d^-1 = 29.87 d = 716.9 h; growth_rate_per_h = 0.0232 d^-1 / 24 = 0.000967 h^-1. Substrate rates are VOLUMETRIC (kgN/m3/d), not biomass-specific (no gDW/reactor-volume conversion given). Stoichiometric ratios DeltaNO2-/DeltaNH4+=1.36, DeltaNO3-/DeltaNH4+=0.12 are directly measured averages. mu_max 0.0984 d^-1 is CITED from ref 31 (Oshiki et al. prior work), not measured here. Ks(NO2-) 0.47 mgN/L measured on planktonic cells this study.
